12:         return if options.include?(key)

14:         attr_reader key
15: 
16:         define_method("#{key}=") do |value|

19:         end
20: 
21:         option_keys << key
22:         defaults[key] = default
23:       end

238:     def normalize_schema_payload(raw_schema)
239:       return nil if raw_schema.nil?
240:       return raw_schema unless raw_schema.is_a?(Hash)
241: 
242:       schema = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_symbolize_keys(raw_schema)
243:       schema_def = extract_schema_definition(schema)
244:       strict = extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
245:       build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)

248:     def extract_schema_definition(schema)
249:       RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema[:schema] || schema)
250:     end
251: 
252:     def extract_schema_strict(schema, schema_def)
253:       return schema[:strict] if schema.key?(:strict)
254:       return schema_def.delete(:strict) if schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
255: 
256:       nil
257:     end

259:     def build_schema_payload(schema, schema_def, strict)
260:       {
261:         name: sanitize_schema_name(schema[:name] || 'response'),
262:         schema: schema_def,
263:         strict: strict.nil? || strict,
264:         description: schema[:description]
265:       }.compact
266:     end

268:     def sanitize_schema_name(name)
269:       sanitized = name.to_s.gsub(/[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]/, '_')
270:       sanitized.empty? ? 'response' : sanitized
271:     end

273:     def add_callback(name, &block)

275:       self
276:     end

278:     def complete_once(&)

281:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message) unless block_given?

283:       normalize_schema_response(response)
284: 
285:       add_message response
286:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, response)
287: 
288:       if response.tool_call?
289:         handle_tool_calls(response, &)
290:       else
291:         response
292:       end
293:     end
294: 
295:     def provider_completion(&)
296:       @provider.complete(
297:         messages,
298:         tools: @tools,
299:         tool_prefs: @tool_prefs,
300:         temperature: @temperature,
301:         model: @model,
302:         params: @params,
303:         headers: @headers,
304:         schema: @schema,
305:         thinking: @thinking,
306:         citations: @citations,
307:         protocol: @protocol,
308:         &wrap_streaming_block(&)
309:       )
310:     end

312:     def normalize_schema_response(response)
313:       return unless @schema && response.content.is_a?(String) && !response.tool_call?
314: 
315:       response.content = JSON.parse(response.content)
316:     rescue JSON::ParserError
317:       # If parsing fails, keep content as string.
318:     end

320:     def set_legacy_callback(name, legacy_name, additive_name, &block)
321:       warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name) if block
322: 
323:       @on[name] = block
324:       self
325:     end
326: 
327:     def warn_legacy_callback_deprecation(legacy_name, additive_name)
328:       RubyLLM.deprecator.warn(
329:         "`#{legacy_name}` is deprecated and will be removed in RubyLLM 2.0. " \
330:         "Use `#{additive_name}` instead."
331:       )
332:     end
333: 
334:     def run_callbacks(name, legacy_name, *args)
335:       @callbacks[name].each { |callback| callback.call(*args) }
336:       @on[legacy_name]&.call(*args)
337:     end
338: 
339:     def wrap_streaming_block(&block)
340:       return nil unless block
341: 
342:       run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
343: 
344:       block
345:     end
346: 
347:     def handle_tool_calls(response, &)
348:       halt_result = if concurrency
349:                       handle_concurrent_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
350:                     else
351:                       handle_sequential_tool_calls(response.tool_calls)
352:                     end
353: 
354:       reset_tool_choice if forced_tool_choice?
355:       halt_result || complete(&)
356:     end
357: 
358:     def handle_sequential_tool_calls(tool_calls)
359:       halt_result = nil
360: 
361:       tool_calls.each_value do |tool_call|
362:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
363:         result = execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
364:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
365:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
366:       end
367: 
368:       halt_result
369:     end
370: 
371:     def handle_concurrent_tool_calls(tool_calls)
372:       halt_result = nil
373: 
374:       execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls) do |tool_call, result|
375:         run_callbacks(:before_message, :new_message)
376:         add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
377:         halt_result = result if result.is_a?(Tool::Halt)
378:       end
379: 
380:       halt_result
381:     end
382: 
383:     def execute_tools_concurrently(tool_calls, &on_result)
384:       ToolConcurrency.run(concurrency, tool_calls, on_result:) do |tool_call|
385:         execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
386:       end
387:     end
388: 
389:     def execute_tool_with_callbacks(tool_call)
390:       run_callbacks(:before_tool_call, :tool_call, tool_call)
391:       result = execute_tool tool_call
392:       run_callbacks(:after_tool_result, :tool_result, result)
393:       result
394:     end
395: 
396:     def add_tool_result_message(tool_call, result)
397:       tool_payload = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
398:       content = content_like?(tool_payload) ? tool_payload : tool_payload.to_s
399:       message = add_message role: :tool, content:, tool_call_id: tool_call.id
400:       run_callbacks(:after_message, :end_message, message)
401:       message
402:     end
403: 
404:     def execute_tool(tool_call)
405:       tool = tools[tool_call.name.to_sym]
406:       if tool.nil?
407:         return {
408:           error: "Model tried to call unavailable tool `#{tool_call.name}`. " \
409:                  "Available tools: #{tools.keys.to_json}."
410:         }
411:       end
412: 
413:       args = tool_call.arguments
414:       payload = {
415:         chat: self,
416:         provider: @provider.slug,
417:         provider_class: @provider.class.name,
418:         model: @model.id,
419:         model_info: @model,
420:         tool: tool,
421:         tool_call: tool_call,
422:         tool_name: tool.name,
423:         tool_arguments: args,
424:         tool_call_id: tool_call.id
425:       }
426: 
427:       RubyLLM.instrument('tool_call.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do |event|
428:         result = tool.call(args)
429:         event[:result] = result
430:         event[:result_content] = result.is_a?(Tool::Halt) ? result.content : result
431:         event[:result_class] = result.class.name
432:         result
433:       end
434:     end
435: 
436:     def update_tool_options(choice:, calls:)
437:       unless choice.nil?
438:         normalized_choice = normalize_tool_choice(choice)
439:         valid_tool_choices = %i[auto none required] + tools.keys
440:         unless valid_tool_choices.include?(normalized_choice)
441:           raise InvalidToolChoiceError,
442:                 "Invalid tool choice: #{choice}. Valid choices are: #{valid_tool_choices.join(', ')}"
443:         end
444: 
445:         @tool_prefs[:choice] = normalized_choice
446:       end
447: 
448:       @tool_prefs[:calls] = normalize_calls(calls) unless calls.nil?
449:     end
450: 
451:     def update_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
452:       @concurrency = normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
453:     end
454: 
455:     def normalize_tool_concurrency(concurrency)
456:       return nil if concurrency.nil? || concurrency == false
457:       return :threads if concurrency == true
458: 
459:       normalized = concurrency.to_sym
460:       return normalized if ToolConcurrency::MODES.include?(normalized)
461: 
462:       raise ArgumentError,
463:             "Unknown tool concurrency: #{concurrency.inspect}. " \
464:             "Available modes: #{ToolConcurrency::MODES.join(', ')}"
465:     end
466: 
467:     def normalize_calls(calls)
468:       case calls
469:       when :many, 'many'
470:         :many
471:       when :one, 'one', 1
472:         :one
473:       else
474:         raise ArgumentError, "Invalid calls value: #{calls.inspect}. Valid values are: :many, :one, or 1"
475:       end
476:     end
477: 
478:     def normalize_tool_choice(choice)
479:       return choice.to_sym if choice.is_a?(String) || choice.is_a?(Symbol)
480:       return tool_name_for_choice_class(choice) if choice.is_a?(Class)
481: 
482:       choice.respond_to?(:name) ? choice.name.to_sym : choice.to_sym
483:     end
484: 
485:     def tool_name_for_choice_class(tool_class)
486:       matched_tool_name = tools.find { |_name, tool| tool.is_a?(tool_class) }&.first
487:       return matched_tool_name if matched_tool_name
488: 
489:       classify_tool_name(tool_class.name)
490:     end
491: 
492:     def classify_tool_name(class_name)
493:       Utils.underscore(class_name.split('::').last).delete_suffix('_tool').to_sym
494:     end
495: 
496:     def forced_tool_choice?
497:       @tool_prefs[:choice] && !%i[auto none].include?(@tool_prefs[:choice])
498:     end
499: 
500:     def reset_tool_choice
501:       @tool_prefs[:choice] = nil
502:     end
503: 
504:     def build_content(message, attachments)
505:       return message if content_like?(message)
506: 
507:       Content.new(message, attachments)
508:     end
509: 
510:     def content_like?(object)
511:       object.is_a?(Content) || object.is_a?(Content::Raw)
512:     end
513: 
514:     def append_system_instruction(instructions)
515:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
516:       system_messages << Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)
517:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages
518:     end
519: 
520:     def replace_system_instruction(instructions)
521:       system_messages, non_system_messages = @messages.partition { |msg| msg.role == :system }
522: 
523:       if system_messages.empty?
524:         system_messages = [Message.new(role: :system, content: instructions)]
525:       else
526:         system_messages.first.content = instructions
527:         system_messages = [system_messages.first]
528:       end
529: 
530:       @messages = system_messages + non_system_messages

85:         models = JSON.parse(data, symbolize_names: true).map { |model| Model::Info.new(model) }

99:       # Fetches and merges the latest models. Call save_to_json when the
100:       # refreshed registry should also be persisted.
101:       def fetch_merged_models(remote_only: false)
102:         RubyLLM.instrument('models.refresh.ruby_llm', remote_only:) do |payload|
103:           existing_models = read_existing_models
104: 
105:           provider_fetch = fetch_provider_models(remote_only: remote_only)
106:           log_provider_fetch(provider_fetch)
107: 
108:           models_dev_fetch = fetch_models_dev_models(existing_models)
109:           log_models_dev_fetch(models_dev_fetch)
110: 
111:           merged_models = merge_with_existing(existing_models, provider_fetch, models_dev_fetch)
112:           payload[:model_count] = merged_models.size
113:           merged_models
114:         end
115:       end

117:       def fetch_provider_models(remote_only: true)
118:         config = RubyLLM.config
119:         providers = remote_only ? Provider.configured_remote_providers(config) : Provider.configured_providers(config)
120:         result = { models: [], fetched_providers: [], configured_names: providers.map(&:name), failed: [] }
121: 
122:         providers.each do |provider_class|
123:           result[:models].concat(provider_class.new(config).list_models)
124:           result[:fetched_providers] << provider_class.slug
125:         rescue StandardError => e
126:           result[:failed] << { name: provider_class.name, slug: provider_class.slug, error: e }
127:         end
128: 
129:         result
130:       end

132:       def resolve(model_id, provider: nil, assume_exists: false, config: nil) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
133:         config ||= RubyLLM.config
134:         provider_class = provider ? Provider.providers[provider.to_sym] : nil
135:         assume_exists = true if provider_class&.local? || provider_class&.assume_models_exist?
136: 
137:         if assume_exists
138:           raise ArgumentError, 'Provider must be specified if assume_exists is true' unless provider
139: 
140:           provider_class ||= raise_unknown_provider(provider)
141: 
142:           model = if provider_class.local?
143:                     begin
144:                       Models.find(model_id, provider)
145:                     rescue ModelNotFoundError
146:                       nil
147:                     end
148:                   end
149: 
150:           model ||= Model::Info.default(model_id, provider_class.slug)
151:         else
152:           model = Models.find model_id, provider
153:           provider_class = Provider.providers[model.provider.to_sym] || raise_unknown_provider(model.provider)
154:         end
155:         [model, provider_class.new(config)]
156:       end

158:       def fetch_models_dev_models(existing_models) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
159:         RubyLLM.logger.info 'Fetching models from models.dev API...'
160: 
161:         connection = Connection.basic do |f|
162:           f.request :json
163:           f.response :json, parser_options: { symbolize_names: true }
164:         end
165:         response = connection.get 'https://models.dev/api.json'
166:         providers = response.body || {}
167: 
168:         models = providers.flat_map do |provider_key, provider_data|
169:           provider_slug = MODELS_DEV_PROVIDER_MAP[provider_key.to_s]
170:           next [] unless provider_slug
171: 
172:           (provider_data[:models] || {}).values.map do |model_data|
173:             Model::Info.new(models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key.to_s))
174:           end
175:         end
176:         { models: models.reject { |model| model.provider.nil? || model.id.nil? }, fetched: true }
177:       rescue StandardError => e
178:         RubyLLM.logger.warn("Failed to fetch models.dev (#{e.class}: #{e.message}). Keeping existing.")
179:         {
180:           models: existing_models.select { |model| model.metadata[:source] == 'models.dev' },
181:           fetched: false
182:         }
183:       end

185:       def read_existing_models
186:         existing_models = instance&.all
187:         existing_models = read_from_json if existing_models.nil? || existing_models.empty?
188:         existing_models
189:       end

191:       def raise_unknown_provider(provider)
192:         available = Provider.providers.keys.join(', ')
193:         raise Error, "Unknown provider: #{provider.inspect}. Available providers: #{available}"
194:       end

196:       def log_provider_fetch(provider_fetch)
197:         RubyLLM.logger.info "Fetching models from providers: #{provider_fetch[:configured_names].join(', ')}"
198:         provider_fetch[:failed].each do |failure|
199:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
200:             "Failed to fetch #{failure[:name]} models (#{failure[:error].class}: #{failure[:error].message}). " \
201:             'Keeping existing.'
202:           )
203:         end
204:       end

206:       def log_models_dev_fetch(models_dev_fetch)
207:         return if models_dev_fetch[:fetched]
208: 
209:         RubyLLM.logger.warn('Using cached models.dev data due to fetch failure.')
210:       end

212:       def merge_with_existing(existing_models, provider_fetch, models_dev_fetch)
213:         existing_by_provider = existing_models.group_by(&:provider)
214:         preserved_models = existing_by_provider
215:                            .except(*provider_fetch[:fetched_providers])
216:                            .values
217:                            .flatten
218: 
219:         provider_models = provider_fetch[:models] + preserved_models
220:         models_dev_models = if models_dev_fetch[:fetched]
221:                               models_dev_fetch[:models]
222:                             else
223:                               existing_models.select { |model| model.metadata[:source] == 'models.dev' }
224:                             end
225: 
226:         merge_models(provider_models, models_dev_models)
227:       end

229:       def merge_models(provider_models, models_dev_models)
230:         models_dev_by_key = index_by_key(models_dev_models)
231:         provider_by_key = index_by_key(provider_models)
232: 
233:         all_keys = models_dev_by_key.keys | provider_by_key.keys
234: 
235:         models = all_keys.map do |key|
236:           models_dev_model = find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
237:           provider_model = provider_by_key[key]
238: 
239:           if models_dev_model && provider_model
240:             add_provider_metadata(models_dev_model, provider_model)
241:           elsif models_dev_model
242:             models_dev_model
243:           else
244:             provider_model
245:           end
246:         end
247: 
248:         filter_models(models).sort_by { |m| [m.provider, m.id] }
249:       end

251:       def filter_models(models)
252:         models.reject do |model|
253:           model.provider.to_s == 'vertexai' && model.id.to_s.include?('/')
254:         end
255:       end
256: 
257:       def find_models_dev_model(key, models_dev_by_key)
258:         # Direct match
259:         return models_dev_by_key[key] if models_dev_by_key[key]
260: 
261:         provider, model_id = key.split(':', 2)
262:         if provider == 'bedrock'
263:           normalized_id = model_id.sub(/^[a-z]{2}\./, '')
264:           context_override = nil
265:           normalized_id = normalized_id.gsub(/:(\d+)k\b/) do
266:             context_override = Regexp.last_match(1).to_i * 1000
267:             ''
268:           end
269:           bedrock_model = models_dev_by_key["bedrock:#{normalized_id}"]
270:           if bedrock_model
271:             data = bedrock_model.to_h.merge(id: model_id)
272:             data[:context_window] = context_override if context_override
273:             return Model::Info.new(data)
274:           end
275:         end
276: 
277:         # VertexAI uses same models as Gemini
278:         return unless provider == 'vertexai'
279: 
280:         gemini_model = models_dev_by_key["gemini:#{model_id}"]
281:         return unless gemini_model
282: 
283:         # Return Gemini's models.dev data but with VertexAI as provider
284:         Model::Info.new(gemini_model.to_h.merge(provider: 'vertexai'))
285:       end
286: 
287:       def index_by_key(models)
288:         models.to_h do |model|
289:           ["#{model.provider}:#{model.id}", model]
290:         end
291:       end

293:       def add_provider_metadata(models_dev_model, provider_model) # rubocop:disable Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
294:         data = models_dev_model.to_h
295:         data[:name] = provider_model.name if blank_value?(data[:name])
296:         data[:family] = provider_model.family if blank_value?(data[:family])
297:         data[:created_at] = provider_model.created_at if blank_value?(data[:created_at])
298:         data[:context_window] = provider_model.context_window if blank_value?(data[:context_window])
299:         data[:max_output_tokens] = provider_model.max_output_tokens if blank_value?(data[:max_output_tokens])
300:         data[:modalities] = provider_model.modalities.to_h if blank_value?(data[:modalities])
301:         data[:pricing] = provider_model.pricing.to_h if blank_value?(data[:pricing])
302:         data[:metadata] = provider_model.metadata.merge(data[:metadata] || {})
303:         provider_capabilities = provider_model.capabilities - MODELS_DEV_AUTHORITY_CAPABILITIES
304:         data[:capabilities] = (models_dev_model.capabilities + provider_capabilities).uniq
305:         normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
306:         Model::Info.new(data)
307:       end
308: 
309:       def normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
310:         return unless data[:id].to_s.include?('embedding')
311: 
312:         modalities = data[:modalities].to_h
313:         modalities[:input] = ['text'] if modalities[:input].nil? || modalities[:input].empty?
314:         modalities[:output] = ['embeddings']
315:         data[:modalities] = modalities
316:       end
317: 
318:       def blank_value?(value)
319:         return true if value.nil?
320:         return value.empty? if value.is_a?(String) || value.is_a?(Array)
321: 
322:         if value.is_a?(Hash)
323:           return true if value.empty?
324: 
325:           return value.values.all? { |nested| blank_value?(nested) }
326:         end
327: 
328:         false
329:       end
330: 
331:       def models_dev_model_to_info(model_data, provider_slug, provider_key)
332:         modalities = normalize_models_dev_modalities(model_data[:modalities])
333:         capabilities = models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
334: 
335:         created_date = [model_data[:release_date], model_data[:last_updated]]
336:                        .find { |value| !value.to_s.strip.empty? }
337: 
338:         data = {
339:           id: model_data[:id],
340:           name: model_data[:name] || model_data[:id],
341:           provider: provider_slug,
342:           family: model_data[:family],
343:           created_at: Utils.iso_date_prefix_to_utc_midnight_string(created_date),
344:           context_window: model_data.dig(:limit, :context),
345:           max_output_tokens: model_data.dig(:limit, :output),
346:           knowledge_cutoff: normalize_models_dev_knowledge(model_data[:knowledge]),
347:           modalities: modalities,
348:           capabilities: capabilities,
349:           pricing: models_dev_pricing(model_data[:cost]),
350:           metadata: models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
351:         }
352: 
353:         normalize_embedding_modalities(data)
354:         data
355:       end
356: 
357:       def models_dev_capabilities(model_data, modalities)
358:         capabilities = []
359:         capabilities << 'function_calling' if model_data[:tool_call]
360:         capabilities << 'structured_output' if model_data[:structured_output]
361:         capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_data[:reasoning] || model_data[:reasoning_options]
362:         capabilities << 'vision' if modalities[:input].intersect?(%w[image video pdf])
363:         capabilities.uniq
364:       end
365: 
366:       def models_dev_pricing(cost)
367:         return {} unless cost
368: 
369:         text_standard = {
370:           input_per_million: cost[:input],
371:           output_per_million: cost[:output],
372:           cache_read_input_per_million: cost[:cache_read],
373:           cache_write_input_per_million: cost[:cache_write],
374:           reasoning_output_per_million: cost[:reasoning]
375:         }.compact
376: 
377:         audio_standard = {
378:           input_per_million: cost[:input_audio],
379:           output_per_million: cost[:output_audio]
380:         }.compact
381: 
382:         pricing = {}
383:         pricing[:text_tokens] = { standard: text_standard } if text_standard.any?
384:         pricing[:audio_tokens] = { standard: audio_standard } if audio_standard.any?
385:         pricing
386:       end
387: 
388:       def models_dev_metadata(model_data, provider_key)
389:         metadata = {
390:           source: 'models.dev',
391:           provider_id: provider_key,
392:           open_weights: model_data[:open_weights],
393:           attachment: model_data[:attachment],
394:           temperature: model_data[:temperature],
395:           last_updated: model_data[:last_updated],
396:           status: model_data[:status],
397:           interleaved: model_data[:interleaved],
398:           reasoning_options: model_data[:reasoning_options],
399:           cost: model_data[:cost],
400:           limit: model_data[:limit],
401:           knowledge: model_data[:knowledge]
402:         }
403:         metadata.compact
404:       end
405: 
406:       def normalize_models_dev_modalities(modalities)
407:         normalized = { input: [], output: [] }
408:         return normalized unless modalities
409: 
410:         normalized[:input] = Array(modalities[:input]).compact & MODELS_DEV_INPUT_MODALITIES
411:         normalized[:output] = Array(modalities[:output]).compact & MODELS_DEV_OUTPUT_MODALITIES
412:         normalized
413:       end
414: 
415:       def normalize_models_dev_knowledge(value)
416:         return if value.nil?
417:         return value if value.is_a?(Date)
418: 
419:         Date.parse(value.to_s)
420:       rescue ArgumentError
421:         nil
422:       end
423:     end

492:       self.class.resolve(model_id, provider: provider, assume_exists: assume_exists, config: config)

497:     def find_with_provider(model_id, provider)
498:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id, provider)
499:       resolved_id = resolve_bedrock_region_id(resolved_id) if provider.to_s == 'bedrock'
500:       all.find { |m| m.id == resolved_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
501:         all.find { |m| m.id == model_id && m.provider == provider.to_s } ||
502:         raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: provider)
503:     end
504: 
505:     def resolve_bedrock_region_id(model_id)
506:       region = RubyLLM.config.bedrock_region.to_s
507:       return model_id if region.empty?
508: 
509:       candidate_id = Providers::Bedrock::Models.with_region_prefix(model_id, region)
510:       return model_id if candidate_id == model_id
511: 
512:       candidate = all.find { |m| m.provider == 'bedrock' && m.id == candidate_id }
513:       return model_id unless candidate
514: 
515:       inference_types = Array(candidate.metadata[:inference_types] || candidate.metadata['inference_types'])
516:       Providers::Bedrock::Models.normalize_inference_profile_id(model_id, inference_types, region)
517:     end
518: 
519:     def find_without_provider(model_id)
520:       exact_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == model_id }
521:       return preferred_match(exact_matches) if exact_matches.any?
522: 
523:       resolved_id = Aliases.resolve(model_id)
524:       alias_matches = all.select { |m| m.id == resolved_id }
525:       return preferred_match(alias_matches) if alias_matches.any?
526: 
527:       raise_model_not_found(model_id)
528:     end
529: 
530:     def raise_model_not_found(model_id, provider: nil)
531:       message = "Unknown model: #{model_id.inspect}"
532:       message = "#{message} for provider: #{provider.inspect}" if provider
533: 
534:       raise ModelNotFoundError, "#{message}. #{refresh_registry_guidance}"
535:     end
536: 
537:     def refresh_registry_guidance
538:       rails_model = RubyLLM.config.model_registry_class
539:       'If the model exists at the provider, refresh the registry with `RubyLLM.models.refresh!` ' \
540:         'and persist it with `RubyLLM.models.save_to_json`. ' \
541:         "Rails model registries can call `#{rails_model}.refresh!` instead."
542:     end
543: 
544:     def preferred_match(candidates)
545:       return candidates.first if candidates.size == 1
546: 
547:       candidates.min_by do |model|
548:         index = PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.index(model.provider)
549:         index || PROVIDER_PREFERENCE.length
550:       end
551:     end
552:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Custom error class that wraps API errors from different providers
5:   # into a consistent format with helpful error messages.
6:   class Error < StandardError
7:     attr_reader :response
8: 
9:     def self.default_message
10:       nil
11:     end
12: 
13:     def initialize(response = nil, message = nil)
14:       if response.is_a?(String)
15:         message = response
16:         response = nil
17:       end
18: 
19:       @response = response
20:       super(message || response&.body || self.class.default_message)
21:     end

3: require 'faraday'

9: module RubyLLM
10:   # Connection class for managing API connections to various providers.
11:   class Connection
12:     attr_reader :provider, :connection, :config
13: 
14:     def self.basic(&)
15:       Faraday.new do |f|
16:         f.response :logger,
17:                    RubyLLM.logger,
18:                    bodies: false,
19:                    errors: true,
20:                    headers: false,
21:                    log_level: :debug
22:         f.response :raise_error
23:         yield f if block_given?
24:       end
25:     end
26: 
27:     def initialize(provider, config)
28:       @provider = provider
29:       @config = config
30: 
31:       @connection = Faraday.new(provider.api_base) do |faraday|
32:         setup_timeout(faraday)
33:         setup_logging(faraday)
34:         setup_retry(faraday)
35:         setup_middleware(faraday)
36:         setup_http_proxy(faraday)
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def post(url, payload, &)
41:       instrument_request(:post, url) do
42:         @connection.post url, payload do |req|
43:           req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
44:           yield req if block_given?
45:         end
46:       end
47:     end
48: 
49:     def get(url, &)
50:       instrument_request(:get, url) do
51:         @connection.get url do |req|
52:           req.headers.merge! @provider.headers
53:           yield req if block_given?
54:         end
55:       end
56:     end
57: 
58:     # Keeps the config and Faraday internals out of pretty-printed output.
59:     def pretty_print_instance_variables
60:       super - %i[@config @connection]
61:     end
62: 
63:     private
64: 
65:     def instrument_request(method, url)
66:       payload = {
67:         provider: @provider.slug,
68:         method: method,
69:         url: url
70:       }
71: 
72:       RubyLLM.instrument('request.ruby_llm', payload, config: @config) do
73:         response = yield
74:         payload[:status] = response.status if response.respond_to?(:status)
75:         response
76:       end
77:     end
78: 
79:     def setup_timeout(faraday)
80:       faraday.options.timeout = @config.request_timeout
81:     end
82: 
83:     def setup_logging(faraday)
84:       faraday.response :logger,
85:                        RubyLLM.logger,
86:                        bodies: RubyLLM.logger.debug?,
87:                        errors: true,
88:                        headers: false,
89:                        log_level: :debug do |logger|
90:         logger.filter(logging_regexp('[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{100,}'), '[BASE64 DATA]')
91:         logger.filter(logging_regexp('[-\\d.e,\\s]{100,}'), '[EMBEDDINGS ARRAY]')
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def logging_regexp(pattern)
96:       return Regexp.new(pattern) if @config.log_regexp_timeout.nil? || !Regexp.respond_to?(:timeout)
97: 
98:       Regexp.new(pattern, timeout: @config.log_regexp_timeout)
99:     end
100: 
101:     def setup_retry(faraday)
102:       faraday.request :retry, {
103:         max: @config.max_retries,
104:         interval: @config.retry_interval,
105:         interval_randomness: @config.retry_interval_randomness,
106:         backoff_factor: @config.retry_backoff_factor,
107:         methods: Faraday::Retry::Middleware::IDEMPOTENT_METHODS + [:post],
108:         exceptions: retry_exceptions
109:       }
110:     end
111: 
112:     def setup_middleware(faraday)
113:       faraday.request :multipart
114:       faraday.request :json
115:       faraday.response :json
116:       faraday.adapter(@config.faraday_adapter)
117:       faraday.use :llm_errors, provider: @provider
118:     end
119: 
120:     def setup_http_proxy(faraday)
121:       return unless @config.http_proxy
122: 
123:       faraday.proxy = @config.http_proxy
124:     end
125:

127:       [
128:         Errno::ETIMEDOUT,
129:         Timeout::Error,
130:         Faraday::TimeoutError,
131:         Faraday::ConnectionFailed,
132:         Faraday::RetriableResponse,
133:         RubyLLM::RateLimitError,
134:         RubyLLM::ServerError,
135:         RubyLLM::ServiceUnavailableError,
136:         RubyLLM::OverloadedError
137:       ]
138:     end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'faraday'
4: require 'ruby_llm/error'
5: 
6: module RubyLLM
7:   # Faraday middleware that maps provider-specific API errors to RubyLLM errors.
8:   class ErrorMiddleware < Faraday::Middleware
9:     def initialize(app, options = {})
10:       super(app)
11:       @provider = options[:provider]
12:     end
13: 
14:     def call(env)
15:       @app.call(env).on_complete do |response|
16:         self.class.parse_error(provider: @provider, response: response)
17:       end
18:     end
19: 
20:     class << self
21:       CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS = [
22:         /context length/i,
23:         /context window/i,
24:         /maximum context/i,
25:         /request too large/i,
26:         /too many tokens/i,
27:         /token count exceeds/i,
28:         /input[_\s-]?token/i,
29:         /input or output tokens? must be reduced/i,
30:         /reduce the length of messages/i,
31:         /prompt is too long/i
32:       ].freeze
33: 
34:       RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS = [
35:         /rate limit/i,
36:         /per minute/i,
37:         /per hour/i,
38:         /per day/i
39:       ].freeze
40: 
41:       def parse_error(provider:, response:)
42:         message = provider&.parse_error(response)
43: 
44:         case response.status
45:         when 200..399
46:           message
47:         when 400
48:           raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
49: 
50:           raise BadRequestError.new(response, message)
51:         when 401
52:           raise UnauthorizedError.new(response, message)
53:         when 402
54:           raise PaymentRequiredError.new(response, message)
55:         when 403
56:           raise ForbiddenError.new(response, message)
57:         when 429
58:           raise RateLimitError.new(response, message) if rate_limited?(message)
59:           raise ContextLengthExceededError.new(response, message) if context_length_exceeded?(message)
60: 
61:           raise RateLimitError.new(response, message)
62:         when 500
63:           raise ServerError.new(response, message)
64:         when 502..504
65:           raise ServiceUnavailableError.new(response, message)
66:         when 529
67:           raise OverloadedError.new(response, message)
68:         else
69:           raise Error.new(response, message)
70:         end
71:       end
72: 
73:       private
74: 
75:       def context_length_exceeded?(message)
76:         return false if message.to_s.empty?
77: 
78:         CONTEXT_LENGTH_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
79:       end
80: 
81:       def rate_limited?(message)
82:         return false if message.to_s.empty?
83: 
84:         RATE_LIMIT_PATTERNS.any? { |pattern| message.match?(pattern) }
85:       end
86:     end
87:   end
88: end
89: 
90: Faraday::Middleware.register_middleware(llm_errors: RubyLLM::ErrorMiddleware)

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     # OpenRouter API integration.
6:     class OpenRouter < Provider
7:       # OpenRouter's dialect of the Chat Completions API.
8:       class ChatCompletions < Protocols::ChatCompletions
9:         include OpenRouter::Chat
10:         include OpenRouter::Images
11:         include OpenRouter::Models
12:         include OpenRouter::Streaming

18:         @config.openrouter_api_base || 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1'
19:       end
20: 
21:       def headers
22:         {
23:           'Authorization' => "Bearer #{@config.openrouter_api_key}"
24:         }
25:       end
26: 
27:       def parse_error(response)
28:         return if response.body.empty?
29: 
30:         body = try_parse_json(response.body)
31:         case body
32:         when Hash
33:           parse_error_part_message body
34:         when Array
35:           body.map do |part|
36:             parse_error_part_message part
37:           end.join('. ')
38:         else
39:           body
40:         end
41:       end

55:       def parse_error_part_message(part)
56:         message = part.dig('error', 'message')
57:         raw = try_parse_json(part.dig('error', 'metadata', 'raw'))
58:         return message unless raw.is_a?(Hash)
59: 
60:         raw_message = raw.dig('error', 'message')
61:         return [message, raw_message].compact.join(' - ') if raw_message
62: 
63:         message
64:       end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Chat methods of the OpenAI API integration
7:       module Chat
8:         def completion_url
9:           'chat/completions'
10:         end
11: 
12:         module_function
13: 
14:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
15:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
16:                            thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
17:           warn_unsupported_citations(model) if citations && !model.citations?
18:           tool_prefs ||= {}
19:           payload = {
20:             model: model.id,
21:             messages: format_messages(messages),
22:             stream: stream
23:           }
24: 
25:           payload[:temperature] = temperature unless temperature.nil?
26:           if tools.any?
27:             payload[:tools] = tools.map { |_, tool| tool_for(tool) }
28:             payload[:tool_choice] = build_tool_choice(tool_prefs[:choice]) unless tool_prefs[:choice].nil?
29:             payload[:parallel_tool_calls] = tool_prefs[:calls] == :many unless tool_prefs[:calls].nil?
30:           end
31: 
32:           if schema
33:             schema_name = schema[:name]
34:             schema_def = schema[:schema]
35:             strict = schema[:strict]
36: 
37:             payload[:response_format] = {
38:               type: 'json_schema',
39:               json_schema: {
40:                 name: schema_name,
41:                 schema: schema_def,
42:                 strict: strict
43:               }
44:             }
45:           end
46: 
47:           effort = resolve_effort(thinking)
48:           payload[:reasoning_effort] = effort if effort
49: 
50:           payload[:stream_options] = { include_usage: true } if stream
51:           payload
52:         end
53:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists,Metrics/PerceivedComplexity
54: 
55:         def warn_unsupported_citations(model)
56:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
57:             "#{model.id} does not support citations according to the model registry. " \
58:             'with_citations may have no effect.'
59:           )
60:         end
61: 
62:         def parse_completion_response(response)
63:           data = response.body
64:           return if data.nil? || data.empty?
65: 
66:           raise Error.new(response, data.dig('error', 'message')) if data.dig('error', 'message')
67: 
68:           message_data = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
69:           return unless message_data
70: 
71:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
72:           thinking_tokens = thinking_tokens(usage)
73:           content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(message_data['content'])
74:           thinking_text = thinking_from_blocks || extract_thinking_text(message_data)
75:           thinking_signature = extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
76: 
77:           Message.new(
78:             role: :assistant,
79:             content: content,
80:             citations: extract_citations(message_data, data, content),
81:             thinking: Thinking.build(text: thinking_text, signature: thinking_signature),
82:             tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(message_data['tool_calls']),
83:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
84:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
85:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
86:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
87:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens,
88:             model_id: data['model'],
89:             raw: response
90:           )
91:         end
92: 
93:         def input_tokens(usage)
94:           return usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens'] if usage['prompt_cache_miss_tokens']
95: 
96:           prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
97:           return unless prompt_tokens
98: 
99:           [prompt_tokens.to_i - cache_read_tokens(usage).to_i - cache_write_tokens(usage).to_i, 0].max
100:         end
101: 
102:         def output_tokens(usage)
103:           completion_tokens = usage['completion_tokens']
104:           return unless completion_tokens
105: 
106:           completion_tokens = completion_tokens.to_i
107:           generated_tokens = generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
108:           return completion_tokens unless generated_tokens && generated_tokens > completion_tokens
109: 
110:           generated_tokens
111:         end
112: 
113:         def generated_tokens_from_total(usage)
114:           prompt_tokens = usage['prompt_tokens']
115:           total_tokens = usage['total_tokens']
116:           return unless prompt_tokens && total_tokens
117: 
118:           [total_tokens.to_i - prompt_tokens.to_i, 0].max
119:         end
120: 
121:         def cache_read_tokens(usage)
122:           usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cached_tokens') || usage['prompt_cache_hit_tokens']
123:         end
124: 
125:         def cache_write_tokens(usage)
126:           usage.dig('prompt_tokens_details', 'cache_write_tokens') || 0
127:         end
128: 
129:         def thinking_tokens(usage)
130:           usage.dig('completion_tokens_details', 'reasoning_tokens') || usage['reasoning_tokens']
131:         end
132: 
133:         def extract_citations(message_data, data, content)
134:           annotations = parse_annotations(message_data['annotations'], content)
135:           return annotations if annotations.any?
136: 
137:           parse_root_citations(data)
138:         end
139: 
140:         def parse_annotations(annotations, content)
141:           Array(annotations).filter_map do |annotation|
142:             details = annotation['url_citation']
143:             next unless details.is_a?(Hash)
144: 
145:             start_index = details['start_index']
146:             end_index = details['end_index']
147: 
148:             Citation.new(
149:               url: details['url'],
150:               title: details['title'],
151:               text: annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index),
152:               start_index: start_index,
153:               end_index: end_index
154:             )
155:           end
156:         end
157: 
158:         def annotated_text(content, start_index, end_index)
159:           return nil unless content.is_a?(String) && start_index && end_index
160: 
161:           content[start_index...end_index]
162:         end
163: 
164:         # Perplexity and xAI return search citations at the root of the response.
165:         def parse_root_citations(data)
166:           search_results = data['search_results']
167:           return parse_search_results(search_results) if search_results.is_a?(Array) && search_results.any?
168: 
169:           Array(data['citations']).each_with_index.filter_map do |url, index|
170:             Citation.new(url: url, source_index: index) if url.is_a?(String)
171:           end
172:         end
173: 
174:         def parse_search_results(results)
175:           results.each_with_index.filter_map do |result, index|
176:             next unless result.is_a?(Hash)
177: 
178:             Citation.new(
179:               url: result['url'],
180:               title: result['title'],
181:               cited_text: result['snippet'],
182:               source_index: index
183:             )
184:           end
185:         end
186: 
187:         def format_messages(messages)
188:           messages.map do |msg|
189:             {
190:               role: format_role(msg.role),
191:               content: format_message_content(msg),
192:               tool_calls: format_tool_calls(msg.tool_calls),
193:               tool_call_id: msg.tool_call_id
194:             }.compact.merge(format_thinking(msg))
195:           end
196:         end
197: 
198:         def format_message_content(msg)
199:           content = format_content(msg.content)
200:           return '' if content.nil? && thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
201: 
202:           content
203:         end
204: 
205:         def thinking_only_assistant_message?(msg)
206:           msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking && !msg.tool_call?
207:         end
208: 
209:         def format_content(content)
210:           Media.format_content(content)
211:         end
212: 
213:         def format_role(role)
214:           case role
215:           when :system
216:             @config.openai_use_system_role ? 'system' : 'developer'
217:           else
218:             role.to_s
219:           end
220:         end
221: 
222:         def resolve_effort(thinking)
223:           return nil unless thinking
224: 
225:           thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : thinking
226:         end
227: 
228:         def format_thinking(msg)
229:           return {} unless msg.role == :assistant
230: 
231:           thinking = msg.thinking
232:           return {} unless thinking
233: 
234:           payload = {}
235:           if thinking.text
236:             payload[:reasoning] = thinking.text
237:             payload[:reasoning_content] = thinking.text
238:           end
239:           payload[:reasoning_signature] = thinking.signature if thinking.signature
240:           payload
241:         end
242: 
243:         def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
244:           candidate = message_data['reasoning_content'] || message_data['reasoning'] || message_data['thinking']
245:           candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
246:         end
247: 
248:         def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
249:           candidate = message_data['reasoning_signature'] || message_data['signature']
250:           candidate.is_a?(String) ? candidate : nil
251:         end
252: 
253:         def extract_content_and_thinking(content)
254:           return extract_think_tag_content(content) if content.is_a?(String)
255:           return [content, nil] unless content.is_a?(Array)
256: 
257:           text = extract_text_from_blocks(content)
258:           thinking = extract_thinking_from_blocks(content)
259: 
260:           [text.empty? ? nil : text, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
261:         end
262: 
263:         def extract_text_from_blocks(blocks)
264:           blocks.filter_map do |block|
265:             block['text'] if block['type'] == 'text' && block['text'].is_a?(String)
266:           end.join
267:         end
268: 
269:         def extract_thinking_from_blocks(blocks)
270:           blocks.filter_map do |block|
271:             next unless block['type'] == 'thinking'
272: 
273:             extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
274:           end.join
275:         end
276: 
277:         def extract_thinking_text_from_block(block)
278:           thinking_block = block['thinking']
279:           return thinking_block if thinking_block.is_a?(String)
280: 
281:           if thinking_block.is_a?(Array)
282:             return thinking_block.filter_map { |item| item['text'] if item['type'] == 'text' }.join
283:           end
284: 
285:           block['text'] if block['text'].is_a?(String)
286:         end
287: 
288:         def extract_think_tag_content(text)
289:           return [text, nil] unless text.include?('<think>')
290: 
291:           thinking = text.scan(%r{<think>(.*?)</think>}m).join
292:           content = text.gsub(%r{<think>.*?</think>}m, '').strip
293: 
294:           [content.empty? ? nil : content, thinking.empty? ? nil : thinking]
295:         end
296:       end
297:     end
298:   end
299: end

5: module RubyLLM

14:         end

16:         def build_chunk(data)
17:           usage = data['usage'] || {}
18:           delta = data.dig('choices', 0, 'delta') || {}
19:           content_source = delta['content'] || data.dig('choices', 0, 'message', 'content')
20:           content, thinking_from_blocks = extract_content_and_thinking(content_source)
21: 
22:           Chunk.new(
23:             role: :assistant,
24:             model_id: data['model'],
25:             content: content,
26:             citations: extract_chunk_citations(delta, data),
27:             thinking: Thinking.build(
28:               text: thinking_from_blocks || delta['reasoning_content'] || delta['reasoning'],
29:               signature: delta['reasoning_signature']
30:             ),
31:             tool_calls: parse_tool_calls(delta['tool_calls'], parse_arguments: false),
32:             input_tokens: input_tokens(usage),
33:             output_tokens: output_tokens(usage),
34:             cached_tokens: cache_read_tokens(usage),
35:             cache_creation_tokens: cache_write_tokens(usage),
36:             thinking_tokens: thinking_tokens(usage)
37:           )
38:         end

41:           annotations = parse_annotations(delta['annotations'], nil)
42:           return annotations if annotations.any?

44:           parse_root_citations(data)

47:         def parse_streaming_error(data)
48:           error_data = JSON.parse(data)

50: 
51:           case error_data.dig('error', 'type')
52:           when 'server_error'
53:             [500, error_data['error']['message']]
54:           when 'rate_limit_exceeded', 'insufficient_quota'
55:             [429, error_data['error']['message']]
56:           else
57:             [400, error_data['error']['message']]
58:           end
59:         end
60:       end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: require 'json'
4: 
5: module RubyLLM
6:   module Protocols
7:     class ChatCompletions
8:       # Tools methods of the OpenAI API integration
9:       module Tools
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         EMPTY_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA = {
13:           'type' => 'object',
14:           'properties' => {},
15:           'required' => [],
16:           'additionalProperties' => false,
17:           'strict' => true
18:         }.freeze
19: 
20:         def parameters_schema_for(tool)
21:           tool.params_schema ||
22:             schema_from_parameters(tool.parameters)
23:         end
24: 
25:         def schema_from_parameters(parameters)
26:           schema_definition = RubyLLM::Tool::SchemaDefinition.from_parameters(parameters)
27:           schema_definition&.json_schema || EMPTY_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA
28:         end
29: 
30:         def tool_for(tool)
31:           parameters_schema = parameters_schema_for(tool)
32: 
33:           definition = {
34:             type: 'function',
35:             function: {
36:               name: tool.name,
37:               description: tool.description,
38:               parameters: parameters_schema
39:             }
40:           }
41: 
42:           return definition if tool.provider_params.empty?
43: 
44:           RubyLLM::Utils.deep_merge(definition, tool.provider_params)
45:         end
46: 
47:         def format_tool_calls(tool_calls)
48:           return nil unless tool_calls&.any?
49: 
50:           tool_calls.map do |_, tc|
51:             call = {
52:               id: tc.id,
53:               type: 'function',
54:               function: {
55:                 name: tc.name,
56:                 arguments: JSON.generate(tc.arguments)
57:               }
58:             }
59:             if tc.thought_signature
60:               call[:extra_content] = {
61:                 google: { thought_signature: tc.thought_signature }
62:               }
63:             end
64:             call
65:           end
66:         end
67: 
68:         def parse_tool_call_arguments(tool_call)
69:           arguments = tool_call.dig('function', 'arguments')
70: 
71:           if arguments.nil? || arguments.empty?
72:             {}
73:           else
74:             JSON.parse(arguments)
75:           end
76:         end
77: 
78:         def parse_tool_calls(tool_calls, parse_arguments: true)
79:           return nil unless tool_calls&.any?
80: 
81:           tool_calls.to_h do |tc|
82:             [
83:               tc['id'],
84:               ToolCall.new(
85:                 id: tc['id'],
86:                 name: tc.dig('function', 'name'),
87:                 arguments: if parse_arguments
88:                              parse_tool_call_arguments(tc)
89:                            else
90:                              tc.dig('function', 'arguments')
91:                            end,
92:                 thought_signature: extract_tool_call_thought_signature(tc)
93:               )
94:             ]
95:           end
96:         end
97: 
98:         def build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
99:           case tool_choice
100:           when :auto, :none, :required
101:             tool_choice
102:           else
103:             {
104:               type: 'function',
105:               function: {
106:                 name: tool_choice
107:               }
108:             }
109:           end
110:         end
111: 
112:         def extract_tool_call_thought_signature(tool_call)
113:           tool_call.dig('extra_content', 'google', 'thought_signature')
114:         end
115:       end
116:     end
117:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Handles formatting of media content (images, audio) for OpenAI APIs
7:       module Media
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def format_content(content, document_attachments: :pdf, image_attachments: true, audio_attachments: true)
11:           format_parts(content) do |attachment|
12:             format_attachment(
13:               attachment,
14:               document_attachments:,
15:               image_attachments:,
16:               audio_attachments:
17:             )
18:           end
19:         end
20: 
21:         # Shared preamble and attachment loop for OpenAI-compatible providers.
22:         # The block formats a single attachment in the provider's dialect.
23:         def format_parts(content)
24:           if content.is_a?(RubyLLM::Content::Raw)
25:             value = content.value
26:             return value.is_a?(Hash) ? value.to_json : value
27:           end
28:           return content.to_json if content.is_a?(Hash) || content.is_a?(Array)
29:           return content unless content.is_a?(Content)
30: 
31:           parts = []
32:           parts << format_text(content.text) if content.text
33: 
34:           content.attachments.each do |attachment|
35:             parts << yield(attachment)
36:           end
37: 
38:           parts
39:         end
40: 
41:         def format_attachment(attachment, document_attachments:, image_attachments:, audio_attachments:)
42:           case attachment.type
43:           when :image
44:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless image_attachments
45: 
46:             format_image(attachment)
47:           when :audio
48:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless audio_attachments
49: 
50:             format_audio(attachment)
51:           when :pdf, :document
52:             format_document_attachment(attachment, document_attachments)
53:           when :text
54:             format_text_file(attachment)
55:           else
56:             raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
57:           end
58:         end
59: 
60:         def format_image(image)
61:           {
62:             type: 'image_url',
63:             image_url: {
64:               url: image.url? ? image.source.to_s : image.for_llm
65:             }
66:           }
67:         end
68: 
69:         def format_document(document)
70:           {
71:             type: 'file',
72:             file: {
73:               filename: document.filename,
74:               file_data: document.for_llm
75:             }
76:           }
77:         end
78: 
79:         def format_text_file(text_file)
80:           {
81:             type: 'text',
82:             text: text_file.for_llm
83:           }
84:         end
85: 
86:         def format_audio(audio)
87:           {
88:             type: 'input_audio',
89:             input_audio: {
90:               data: audio.encoded,
91:               format: audio.format
92:             }
93:           }
94:         end
95: 
96:         def format_text(text)
97:           {
98:             type: 'text',
99:             text: text
100:           }
101:         end
102: 
103:         def format_document_attachment(attachment, strategy)
104:           return format_document(attachment) if strategy == :all
105:           return format_document(attachment) if strategy == :pdf && attachment.pdf?
106: 
107:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type
108:         end
109:       end
110:     end
111:   end
112: end

31: 
32:           raise Error.new(nil, 'Unexpected response format from OpenAI image API') unless image_data
33:

36:             mime_type: 'image/png', # DALL-E typically returns PNGs
37:             revised_prompt: image_data['revised_prompt'],

39:             data: image_data['b64_json'],
40:             usage: data['usage'] || {}
41:           )
42:         end

45:           return unless editing?(with, mask)
46: 
47:           raise ArgumentError, 'with: is required when mask: is provided' if mask && !attachments?(with)
48:         end
49: 
50:         def render_edit_payload(prompt, model:, with:, mask:, params:)
51:           payload = params.merge(
52:             model: model,
53:             prompt: prompt,
54:             image: build_upload_parts(with),
55:             n: 1
56:           )
57:           payload[:mask] = build_upload_part(mask) if mask
58:           payload
59:         end
60: 
61:         def build_upload_parts(sources)
62:           Array(sources).filter_map do |source|
63:             next if blank_attachment?(source)
64: 
65:             build_upload_part(source)
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         def build_upload_part(source)
70:           attachment = Attachment.new(source)
71:           raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, attachment.mime_type unless attachment.image?
72: 
73:           Faraday::UploadIO.new(StringIO.new(attachment.content), attachment.mime_type, attachment.filename)
74:         end
75: 
76:         def editing?(with, mask)
77:           attachments?(with) || !mask.nil?
78:         end
79: 
80:         def attachments?(value)
81:           Array(value).any? { |item| !blank_attachment?(item) }
82:         end
83: 
84:         def blank_attachment?(value)
85:           value.nil? || (value.is_a?(String) && value.strip.empty?)

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Protocols
5:     class ChatCompletions
6:       # Normalizes temperature for OpenAI models with provider-specific requirements.
7:       module Temperature
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         def normalize(temperature, model_id)
11:           if forced_temperature_model?(model_id) && !temperature.nil? && !temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
12:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} requires temperature=1.0, setting that instead." }
13:             1.0
14:           elsif model_id.include?('-search')
15:             RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Model #{model_id} does not accept temperature parameter, removing" }
16:             nil
17:           else
18:             temperature
19:           end
20:         end
21: 
22:         def temperature_close_to_one?(temperature)
23:           (temperature.to_f - 1.0).abs <= Float::EPSILON
24:         end
25: 
26:         def forced_temperature_model?(model_id)
27:           model_id.match?(/^o\d/) ||                          # o1, o3, o4-mini, etc.
28:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?(-\d{4})?$/) ||    # gpt-5, gpt-5.4, gpt-5.4-2026-03-05
29:             model_id.match?(/^gpt-5(\.\d+)?-pro/)             # gpt-5-pro, gpt-5.4-pro
30:         end
31:       end
32:     end

25:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
26:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false,
27:                            schema: nil, thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
28:           payload = super
29:           payload.delete(:stream_options)
30:           configure_thinking_payload(payload, model, thinking)
31:           normalize_required_tool_choice(payload)
32:           payload
33:         end
34:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
35: 
36:         def build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
37:           return 'any' if tool_choice == :required
38: 
39:           Protocols::ChatCompletions::Tools.build_tool_choice(tool_choice)
40:         end
41: 
42:         def normalize_required_tool_choice(payload)
43:           return unless payload[:tool_choice] == 'any' && Array(payload[:tools]).one?
44: 
45:           function_name = payload.dig(:tools, 0, :function, :name)
46:           return unless function_name
47: 
48:           payload[:tool_choice] = {
49:             type: 'function',
50:             function: { name: function_name }
51:           }
52:         end
53: 
54:         def format_content_with_thinking(msg)
55:           formatted_content = Mistral::Media.format_content(msg.content)
56:           return formatted_content unless msg.role == :assistant && msg.thinking
57: 
58:           content_blocks = build_thinking_blocks(msg.thinking)
59:           append_formatted_content(content_blocks, formatted_content)
60: 
61:           content_blocks
62:         end
63: 
64:         def warn_on_unsupported_thinking(model, thinking)
65:           return unless thinking&.enabled?
66:           return if native_reasoning_model?(model.id) || adjustable_reasoning_model?(model.id)
67: 
68:           RubyLLM.logger.warn(
69:             'Mistral thinking is only supported on Magistral and adjustable-reasoning models. ' \
70:             "Ignoring thinking settings for #{model.id}."
71:           )
72:         end
73: 
74:         def configure_thinking_payload(payload, model, thinking)
75:           return unless thinking&.enabled?
76: 
77:           if native_reasoning_model?(model.id)
78:             configure_native_reasoning_payload(payload, thinking)
79:           elsif adjustable_reasoning_model?(model.id)
80:             payload[:reasoning_effort] = reasoning_effort_for(thinking)
81:           else
82:             payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
83:             warn_on_unsupported_thinking(model, thinking)
84:           end
85:         end
86: 
87:         def configure_native_reasoning_payload(payload, thinking)
88:           payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
89:           payload[:prompt_mode] = thinking.effort == 'none' ? nil : 'reasoning'
90:         end
91: 
92:         def reasoning_effort_for(thinking)
93:           effort = thinking.respond_to?(:effort) ? thinking.effort : nil
94:           return effort if %w[high none].include?(effort)
95: 
96:           'high'
97:         end
98: 
99:         def native_reasoning_model?(model_id)
100:           model_id.to_s.include?('magistral')
101:         end
102: 
103:         def adjustable_reasoning_model?(model_id)
104:           model_id.to_s.match?(/\Amistral-(?:small-latest|medium-(?:3(?:[.-]5)?|latest))\z/)
105:         end
106: 
107:         def build_thinking_blocks(thinking)
108:           return [] unless thinking
109: 
110:           if thinking.text
111:             [{
112:               type: 'thinking',
113:               thinking: [{ type: 'text', text: thinking.text }],
114:               signature: thinking.signature
115:             }.compact]
116:           elsif thinking.signature
117:             [{ type: 'thinking', signature: thinking.signature }]
118:           else
119:             []
120:           end
121:         end
122: 
123:         def append_formatted_content(content_blocks, formatted_content)
124:           if formatted_content.is_a?(Array)
125:             content_blocks.concat(formatted_content)
126:           elsif formatted_content
127:             content_blocks << { type: 'text', text: formatted_content }
128:           end
129:         end
130:       end
131:     end
132:   end
133: end

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class Perplexity
6:       # Provider-level capability checks and narrow registry fallbacks.
7:       module Capabilities
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         PRICES = {
11:           sonar: { input: 1.0, output: 1.0 },
12:           sonar_pro: { input: 3.0, output: 15.0 },
13:           sonar_reasoning: { input: 1.0, output: 5.0 },
14:           sonar_reasoning_pro: { input: 2.0, output: 8.0 },
15:           sonar_deep_research: {
16:             input: 2.0,
17:             output: 8.0,
18:             reasoning_output: 3.0
19:           }
20:         }.freeze
21: 
22:         def supports_tool_choice?(_model_id)
23:           false
24:         end
25: 
26:         def supports_tool_parallel_control?(_model_id)
27:           false
28:         end
29: 
30:         def context_window_for(model_id)
31:           model_id.match?(/sonar-pro/) ? 200_000 : 128_000
32:         end
33: 
34:         def max_tokens_for(model_id)
35:           model_id.match?(/sonar-(?:pro|reasoning-pro)/) ? 8_192 : 4_096
36:         end
37: 
38:         def critical_capabilities_for(model_id)
39:           # Every Perplexity model returns search result citations.
40:           capabilities = ['citations']
41:           capabilities << 'vision' if model_id.match?(/sonar(?:-pro|-reasoning(?:-pro)?)?$/)
42:           capabilities << 'reasoning' if model_id.match?(/reasoning|deep-research/)
43:           capabilities
44:         end
45: 
46:         def pricing_for(model_id)
47:           prices = PRICES.fetch(model_family(model_id), { input: 1.0, output: 1.0 })
48: 
49:           standard = {
50:             input_per_million: prices[:input],
51:             output_per_million: prices[:output]
52:           }
53:           standard[:reasoning_output_per_million] = prices[:reasoning_output] if prices[:reasoning_output]
54: 
55:           { text_tokens: { standard: standard } }
56:         end
57: 
58:         def model_family(model_id)
59:           case model_id
60:           when 'sonar' then :sonar
61:           when 'sonar-pro' then :sonar_pro
62:           when 'sonar-reasoning' then :sonar_reasoning
63:           when 'sonar-reasoning-pro' then :sonar_reasoning_pro
64:           when 'sonar-deep-research' then :sonar_deep_research
65:           else :unknown
66:           end
67:         end
68: 
69:         module_function :context_window_for, :max_tokens_for, :critical_capabilities_for, :pricing_for, :model_family
70:       end
71:     end
72:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   # Base class for wire protocols. A protocol knows how to talk to a family
5:   # of APIs: rendering payloads, parsing responses, streaming chunks, and the
6:   # endpoints involved. Providers know where to talk and who they are.
7:   class Protocol
8:     include Streaming
9: 
10:     attr_reader :provider, :config, :connection, :model
11: 
12:     def initialize(provider, model = nil)
13:       @provider = provider
14:       @config = provider.config
15:       @connection = provider.connection
16:       @model = model
17:     end
18: 
19:     # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
20:     def complete(messages, tools:, temperature:, params: {}, headers: {}, schema: nil, thinking: nil,
21:                  citations: false, tool_prefs: nil, &)
22:       normalized_temperature = maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, model)
23: 
24:       payload = Utils.deep_merge(
25:         render_payload(
26:           messages,
27:           tools: tools,
28:           tool_prefs: tool_prefs,
29:           temperature: normalized_temperature,
30:           model: model,
31:           stream: block_given?,
32:           schema: schema,
33:           thinking: thinking,
34:           citations: citations
35:         ),
36:         params
37:       )
38: 
39:       if block_given?
40:         stream_response payload, headers, &
41:       else
42:         sync_response payload, headers
43:       end
44:     end
45:     # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
46: 
47:     def list_models
48:       response = @connection.get models_url
49:       parse_list_models_response response, @provider.slug, @provider.capabilities
50:     end
51: 
52:     def embed(text, model:, dimensions:)
53:       payload = render_embedding_payload(text, model:, dimensions:)
54:       response = @connection.post(embedding_url(model:), payload)
55:       parse_embedding_response(response, model:, text:)
56:     end
57: 
58:     def moderate(input, model:)
59:       payload = render_moderation_payload(input, model:)
60:       response = @connection.post moderation_url, payload
61:       parse_moderation_response(response, model:)
62:     end
63: 
64:     def paint(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
65:       validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
66:       payload = render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with:, mask:, params:)
67:       response = @connection.post images_url(with:, mask:), payload
68:       parse_image_response(response, model:)
69:     end
70: 
71:     def transcribe(audio_file, model:, language:, **options)
72:       file_part = build_audio_file_part(audio_file)
73:       payload = render_transcription_payload(file_part, model:, language:, **options)
74:       response = @connection.post transcription_url, payload
75:       parse_transcription_response(response, model:)
76:     end
77: 
78:     def maybe_normalize_temperature(temperature, _model)
79:       temperature
80:     end
81: 
82:     def parse_error(response)
83:       @provider.parse_error(response)
84:     end
85: 
86:     private
87: 
88:     def validate_paint_inputs!(with:, mask:)
89:       return if with.nil? && mask.nil?
90: 
91:       raise UnsupportedAttachmentError, 'image reference'
92:     end
93: 
94:     def build_audio_file_part(file_path)
95:       require 'faraday/multipart'
96:       require 'marcel'
97:       require 'pathname'
98: 
99:       expanded_path = File.expand_path(file_path)
100:       mime_type = Marcel::MimeType.for(Pathname.new(expanded_path))
101: 
102:       Faraday::Multipart::FilePart.new(
103:         expanded_path,
104:         mime_type,
105:         File.basename(expanded_path)
106:       )
107:     end
108: 
109:     def sync_response(payload, additional_headers = {})
110:       response = @connection.post completion_url, payload do |req|
111:         req.headers = additional_headers.merge(req.headers) unless additional_headers.empty?
112:       end
113:       parse_completion_response response
114:     end
115:   end
116: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class OpenRouter
6:       # Chat methods of the OpenRouter API integration
7:       module Chat
8:         module_function
9: 
10:         # rubocop:disable Metrics/ParameterLists
11:         def render_payload(messages, tools:, temperature:, model:, stream: false, schema: nil,
12:                            thinking: nil, citations: false, tool_prefs: nil)
13:           payload = super
14:           payload.delete(:reasoning_effort)
15:           strip_schema_strict(payload)
16: 
17:           reasoning = build_reasoning(thinking)
18:           payload[:reasoning] = reasoning if reasoning
19:           payload
20:         end
21:         # rubocop:enable Metrics/ParameterLists
22: 
23:         def strip_schema_strict(payload)
24:           schema_def = payload.dig(:response_format, :json_schema, :schema)
25:           return unless schema_def.is_a?(Hash)
26: 
27:           schema_def = RubyLLM::Utils.deep_dup(schema_def)
28:           schema_def.delete(:strict)
29:           schema_def.delete('strict')
30:           payload[:response_format][:json_schema][:schema] = schema_def
31:         end
32: 
33:         def build_reasoning(thinking)
34:           return nil unless thinking&.enabled?
35: 
36:           reasoning = {}
37:           reasoning[:effort] = thinking.effort if thinking.respond_to?(:effort) && thinking.effort
38:           reasoning[:max_tokens] = thinking.budget if thinking.respond_to?(:budget) && thinking.budget
39:           reasoning[:enabled] = true if reasoning.empty?
40:           reasoning
41:         end
42: 
43:         def format_thinking(msg)
44:           thinking = msg.thinking
45:           return {} unless thinking && msg.role == :assistant
46: 
47:           details = []
48:           if thinking.text
49:             details << {
50:               type: 'reasoning.text',
51:               text: thinking.text,
52:               signature: thinking.signature
53:             }.compact
54:           elsif thinking.signature
55:             details << {
56:               type: 'reasoning.encrypted',
57:               data: thinking.signature
58:             }
59:           end
60: 
61:           details.empty? ? {} : { reasoning_details: details }
62:         end
63: 
64:         def extract_thinking_text(message_data)
65:           candidate = message_data['reasoning']
66:           return candidate if candidate.is_a?(String)
67: 
68:           details = message_data['reasoning_details']
69:           return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)
70: 
71:           text = details.filter_map do |detail|
72:             case detail['type']
73:             when 'reasoning.text'
74:               detail['text']
75:             when 'reasoning.summary'
76:               detail['summary']
77:             end
78:           end.join
79: 
80:           text.empty? ? nil : text
81:         end
82: 
83:         def extract_thinking_signature(message_data)
84:           details = message_data['reasoning_details']
85:           return nil unless details.is_a?(Array)
86: 
87:           signature = details.filter_map do |detail|
88:             detail['signature'] if detail['signature'].is_a?(String)
89:           end.first
90:           return signature if signature
91: 
92:           encrypted = details.find { |detail| detail['type'] == 'reasoning.encrypted' && detail['data'].is_a?(String) }
93:           encrypted&.dig('data')
94:         end
95:       end
96:     end
97:   end
98: end

3: module RubyLLM
4:   module Providers
5:     class OpenRouter
6:       # Image generation methods for the OpenRouter API integration.
7:       # OpenRouter uses the chat completions endpoint for image generation
8:       # instead of a dedicated images endpoint.
9:       module Images
10:         module_function
11: 
12:         def images_url(with: nil, mask: nil) # rubocop:disable Lint/UnusedMethodArgument
13:           'chat/completions'
14:         end
15: 
16:         def render_image_payload(prompt, model:, size:, with: nil, mask: nil, params: {}) # rubocop:disable Lint/UnusedMethodArgument,Metrics/ParameterLists
17:           RubyLLM.logger.debug { "Ignoring size #{size}. OpenRouter image generation does not support size parameter." }
18:           {
19:             model: model,
20:             messages: [
21:               {
22:                 role: 'user',
23:                 content: prompt
24:               }
25:             ],
26:             modalities: %w[image text]
27:           }
28:         end
29: 
30:         def parse_image_response(response, model:)
31:           data = response.body
32:           message = data.dig('choices', 0, 'message')
33: 
34:           unless message&.key?('images') && message['images']&.any?
35:             raise Error.new(nil, 'Unexpected response format from OpenRouter image generation API')
36:           end
37: 
38:           image_data = message['images'].first
39:           image_url = image_data.dig('image_url', 'url') || image_data['url']
40: 
41:           raise Error.new(nil, 'No image URL found in OpenRouter response') unless image_url
42: 
43:           build_image_from_url(image_url, model)
44:         end
45: 
46:         def build_image_from_url(image_url, model)
47:           if image_url.start_with?('data:')
48:             # Parse data URL format: data:image/png;base64,<data>
49:             match = image_url.match(/^data:([^;]+);base64,(.+)$/)
50:             raise Error.new(nil, 'Invalid data URL format from OpenRouter') unless match
51: 
52:             Image.new(
53:               data: match[2],
54:               mime_type: match[1],
55:               model_id: model
56:             )
57:           else
58:             # Regular URL
59:             Image.new(
60:               url: image_url,
61:               mime_type: 'image/png',
62:               model_id: model
63:             )
64:           end
65:         end
66:       end
67:     end
68:   end
69: end
